codeReview.summary: Implementation fully satisfies all requirements for cursor-based pagination. The code is clean, well-tested, and properly integrated with existing filtering capabilities.

summary: Cursor-based pagination for GET /spikes is fully implemented and tested. The implementation uses timestamp-based ordering (WHERE timestamp < ? ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT ?) with support for ?cursor= and ?limit= query parameters. Default limit is 100, maximum is 1000. Response format correctly includes data array and next_cursor field (null when no more results). Invalid limit parameters return 400 VALIDATION_ERROR. Pagination works correctly with all filter parameters (project, page, reviewer, rating). All 12 new pagination tests pass, plus 88 total tests across the worker test suite.
